title: Knysna High School rugby players selected
description: KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna High School is beaming...
bodyText:
KNYSNA NEWS - Knysna High School is beaming with pride as three of its talented rugby players have received invitations to tour Ireland in an exciting opportunity set for September. Ronaldino Hunter, Nico Jafta and Jessie Lottering have been selected to join the prestigious Rhinos Rugby Team on a ten-day tour.


> The selection of these players is a testament to their outstanding abilities and commitment to the sport. These three players have showcased their talent on the rugby field, catching the attention of selectors and earning their place in this esteemed touring team.


Among the trio, Jessie Lottering previously attended a rugby tour to Scotland. His exposure to international rugby and diverse playing styles will undoubtedly contribute to the success of the team.

Lottering's prior tour serves as a foundation for growth and development, preparing him for the challenges and excitement that await in Ireland.

The Rhinos Rugby Team's tour to Ireland has been designed to provide participants with a comprehensive experience of the country's rugby culture and offerings.

Ronaldino Hunter, Nico Jafta and Jessie Lottering are making KHS proud by being selected for an upcoming rugby tour to Ireland in September.

Beyond the sport itself, the tour aims to expose the players to the rich heritage and traditions of Ireland, allowing them to immerse themselves in the local community.


> The Ireland tour goes beyond the rugby field, serving as a gateway to prospective opportunities for the participating players.


By interacting with local teams, coaches and rugby enthusiasts, Ronaldino Hunter, Nico Jafta, and Jessie Lottering will gain insights into different rugby systems, training methodologies and potential pathways for future success.

Read more on www.knysnaplettherald.com

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
